description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by two wide metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood has a weathered, brown appearance with visible grain and subtle color variations, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The metal hoops are silver-gray and fastened with large, round-headed rivets, providing structural reinforcement. The top of the barrel is flat and appears to be made from the same wood as the sides, tightly fitted to prevent spillage. There are no visible handles or spouts, and the surface texture is rough and matte, typical of untreated or lightly finished wood. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage yards, or near workshops, often used for storage or as a decorative element.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops and a flat wooden top, featuring a rough, matte surface and no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat wooden top, metal hoops, and prominent rivets on the left side.
description_view_1: Side view with the metal hoops and rivets more centered, highlighting the barrel's bulging shape.
description_view_2: Another side view with fewer visible rivets, emphasizing the wood grain and curvature.
description_view_3: Rear view with the least visible rivets, showing the continuous wood staves and metal hoops.
